name: Peñacerrada and La Aldea Passes Loop
name_original: Puertos de La Aldea y de Peñacerrada — circular desde Labastida
description: This difficult 55.0-mile road cycling route from Labastida takes you through the scenic Rioja Alavesa, climbing the Ports of La Aldea and Pe
region_intro: Road cycling around Labastida/Bastida is characterized by diverse topography, ranging from the challenging ascents of the Sierra de Cantabria to the rolling vineyards and fertile plains of the Ebro River. This region offers varied terrain for road cyclists, with routes traversing extensive wine-producing areas and historic town centers. The blend of mountains, vineyards, and river landscapes provides a rich topographical experience. Best road cycling routes around Labastida/Bastida The most popular road cycling route is Haro: Peñacerrada and Herrera Passes Loop, a 37.9 miles (61.0 km) trail that takes 3 hours 23 minutes to complete. This difficult route features significant elevation gain through mountainous terrain. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Rioja Alta Loop, a moderate 42.5 miles (68.4 km) path. This route winds through the picturesque Rioja Alta region, offering views of vineyards and rolling hills. Local road cyclists also love the Peñacerrada and La Aldea Passes Loop, a 55.0 miles (88.5 km) trail leading through the Sierra de Cantabria foothills, often completed in about 4 hours 30 minutes. Road cycling around Labastida/Bastida is defined by the Sierra de Cantabria mountain range, extensive vineyards, and the Ebro River plains. The network offers options for various ability levels, from moderate rides through wine country to demanding climbs. The routes in Labastida/Bastida are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.7 stars from over 90 reviews. More than 4,900 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Labastida/Bastida's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of terrain can I expect when road cycling around Labastida/Bastida?
A: The region offers a diverse topographical experience. You'll encounter challenging ascents in the Sierra de Cantabria to the north, rolling vineyards on the lower slopes, and fertile plains along the Ebro River to the south. This blend provides varied landscapes for road cyclists.

Q: Are there road cycling routes suitable for beginners or families in Labastida/Bastida?
A: Yes, Labastida/Bastida offers a range of routes for various skill levels. While some routes feature significant elevation gain, there are also moderate rides through wine country that are more accessible. Out of 107 total road cycling routes, 17 are classified as easy, making them suitable for less experienced riders or families.

Q: What are the typical difficulty levels for road cycling routes in this area?
A: Road cycling routes around Labastida/Bastida range from moderate to difficult. You'll find 54 moderate routes and 36 difficult routes, often featuring significant elevation gains, especially those venturing into the Sierra de Cantabria foothills. An example of a challenging ride is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1035131113/ascent-to-the-balcony-of-la-rioja-and-circular-route-through-the-county-of-trevino-from-labastida" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ascent to the Balcony of La Rioja and circular route through the County of Treviño&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What natural features or landscapes will I see on road cycling routes in Labastida/Bastida?
A: You'll primarily traverse extensive vineyards, which are a defining characteristic of the Rioja Alavesa region. Routes also offer views of the imposing Sierra de Cantabria mountain range and the fertile plains of the Ebro River. The blend of these features creates picturesque and varied scenery.

Q: Are there any notable landmarks or viewpoints along the road cycling routes?
A: Yes, many routes offer scenic viewpoints and pass by historic towns. For instance, you might encounter mountain passes like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2850737" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puerto de Herrera&lt;/a&gt; or enjoy views from spots like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4199700" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cruz de Motrico&lt;/a&gt;. Historic settlements such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5184637" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Haro&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5474818" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Berganzo Village&lt;/a&gt; are also often part of the cycling experience.

Q: What is the best time of year for road cycling in Labastida/Bastida?
A: The region is generally pleasant for cycling for much of the year. Spring and autumn offer mild temperatures and vibrant landscapes, with the vineyards changing colors. Summer can be warm, but early mornings are often ideal. Winter cycling is possible, especially on lower elevation routes, though conditions can vary.

Q: Where can I find parking for my car when starting a road cycling route in Labastida/Bastida?
A: Labastida/Bastida, being a local town, typically offers various parking options within or on the outskirts of the town center. Many routes start from accessible points, and local amenities often include parking. It's advisable to check specific starting points for designated cyclist parking areas.

Q: What do other road cyclists think about the routes in Labastida/Bastida?
A: The road cycling routes in Labastida/Bastida are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.7 stars from over 90 reviews. Cyclists often praise the well-maintained roads, low traffic on many ascents, and the stunning views through vineyards and mountainous terrain.

Q: Are there any circular road cycling routes available?
A: Yes, many of the popular road cycling routes in the area are loops, allowing you to start and finish in the same location. Examples include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e990696986/haro-penacerrada-and-herrera-passes-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Haro: Peñacerrada and Herrera Passes Loop&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e990680785/rioja-alta-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Rioja Alta Loop&lt;/a&gt;, which are designed for a continuous ride back to your starting point.

Q: Can I find cafes or places to stop for refreshments along the cycling routes?
A: Given the region's focus on wine and tourism, many routes pass through or near charming historic town centers and villages. These often provide opportunities to stop at local cafes, restaurants, or bodegas for refreshments and to experience the local culture.

Q: How many road cycling routes are available in the Labastida/Bastida area?
A: There are over 100 road cycling routes available around Labastida/Bastida, with a total of 107 routes listed. This extensive network offers a wide variety of options for different preferences and skill levels.

Q: Which road cycling routes offer significant elevation gain for a challenging ride?
A: For those seeking a challenge, routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1035128078/penacerrada-and-la-aldea-passes-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peñacerrada and La Aldea Passes Loop&lt;/a&gt;, with over 1500 meters of elevation gain, or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1035131113/ascent-to-the-balcony-of-la-rioja-and-circular-route-through-the-county-of-trevino-from-labastida" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ascent to the Balcony of La Rioja&lt;/a&gt;, featuring nearly 1700 meters of climbing, provide demanding experiences through mountainous terrain.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete this road cycling route?
faqs.answer: This challenging route, covering approximately 88.5 kilometers (55 miles) with over 1,500 meters (5,000 feet) of elevation gain, typically takes around 4 hours and 31 minutes for experienced cyclists. Keep in mind that this is an estimated duration and can vary based on your fitness level and stops.

faqs.question: Is this cycling route suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: No, this route is categorized as 'difficult' and requires a very good level of fitness. The significant elevation gain and distance make it unsuitable for beginner cyclists.

faqs.question: Where can I park in Labastida to start this cycling route?
faqs.answer: As the route starts and ends in Labastida, you'll need to find suitable parking within the town. While specific parking areas aren't detailed, Labastida is a well-known starting point for routes in the Rioja Alavesa, so look for public parking options in or near the town center.

faqs.question: What is the terrain and surface like on this route?
faqs.answer: The route primarily consists of well-paved surfaces, offering a smooth ride from a surface quality perspective. However, the significant elevation gain means you'll encounter sustained climbs through mountainous and hilly terrain, including the notable Peñacerrada Pass and Puerto de La Aldea.

faqs.question: What kind of scenery can I expect along the way?
faqs.answer: You can expect diverse and picturesque scenery. The lower slopes feature delightful vineyards characteristic of the Rioja wine region. As you ascend, the route takes on a more mountainous feel with looping corners, offering expansive vistas from higher elevations, especially around the mountain passes. You'll also pass through charming settlements like Peñacerrada and Bernedo Village.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: The best time to cycle this route is generally during spring and autumn when temperatures are milder and the weather is more stable. Summer can be quite hot in the Rioja Alavesa region, especially on exposed climbs, while winter may bring colder temperatures and a higher chance of adverse weather conditions in the mountains.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or entrance fees required for cycling in Rioja Alavesa?
faqs.answer: Generally, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required for road cycling on public roads in the Rioja Alavesa region. However, always respect local regulations, especially when passing through villages or protected areas. It's always a good idea to check for any temporary restrictions or local events before your ride.

faqs.question: Is this cycling route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: This is a challenging road cycling route, and as such, it is not considered dog-friendly. The high speeds, traffic, and physical demands of the route are not suitable for dogs, even if carried in a trailer. For dog-friendly options, consider dedicated walking or less strenuous cycling paths in the region.

faqs.question: Does this route connect with other cycling or hiking trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route briefly intersects with several other paths. You'll encounter short sections of trails such as [CIMA LR12] Rivas de Tereso * Labastida, [CIMA LR12] Rivas de Tereso * San Vicente de la Sonsierra, and the Ignatian Way, among others.

faqs.question: Are there any cafes or places to refuel along the route or in Labastida?
faqs.answer: Labastida, as your starting and ending point, offers various cafes, pubs, and restaurants where you can refuel before or after your ride. Along the route, you'll pass through settlements like Peñacerrada and Bernedo Village, which may have limited options for refreshments. It's advisable to carry sufficient water and snacks, especially given the route's difficulty and length.

faqs.question: What makes the Ports of La Aldea and Peñacerrada particularly notable for cyclists?
faqs.answer: The Puerto de La Aldea and Peñacerrada Pass are considered 'mythical ports' among local cyclists. They are renowned for their significant ascents and the rewarding views they offer from their summits. The route provides a unique opportunity to experience both the cultural vineyard landscapes and the more rugged, mountainous terrain of the Rioja Alavesa.
